merge.diff:
diff --git a/src/jinja2/loaders.py b/src/jinja2/loaders.py
index d7d9bd04..c9fa5d41 100644
--- a/src/jinja2/loaders.py
+++ b/src/jinja2/loaders.py
@@ -170,6 +170,18 @@ class FileSystemLoader(BaseLoader):
     :param encoding: Use this encoding to read the text from template
         files.
     :param followlinks: Follow symbolic links in the path.
+    :param auto_reload: Track the :class:`~jinja2.Environment`
+        instances that use this loader in ``_environments`` so that
+        :meth:`_on_template_changed` can proactively invalidate their
+        cached templates when a search path file changes, rather than
+        waiting for the next time the template is requested. Defaults
+        to ``True``.
+
+    .. versionchanged:: 3.1
+        Added the ``auto_reload`` parameter, along with the
+        ``_environments`` and ``_on_template_changed`` internals used
+        to invalidate cached templates when a search path file
+        changes.
 
     .. versionchanged:: 2.8
         Added the ``followlinks`` parameter.
@@ -180,6 +192,7 @@ class FileSystemLoader(BaseLoader):
         searchpath: t.Union[str, os.PathLike, t.Sequence[t.Union[str, os.PathLike]]],
         encoding: str = "utf-8",
         followlinks: bool = False,
+        auto_reload: bool = True,
     ) -> None:
         if not isinstance(searchpath, abc.Iterable) or isinstance(searchpath, str):
             searchpath = [searchpath]
@@ -187,11 +200,18 @@ class FileSystemLoader(BaseLoader):
         self.searchpath = [os.fspath(p) for p in searchpath]
         self.encoding = encoding
         self.followlinks = followlinks
+        self.auto_reload = auto_reload
+
+        # Environments that load templates through this loader, tracked
+        # with weak references so environments can still be garbage
+        # collected normally. Only populated while ``auto_reload`` is
+        # enabled.
+        self._environments: "weakref.WeakSet[Environment]" = weakref.WeakSet()
 
     def get_source(
         self, environment: "Environment", template: str
     ) -> t.Tuple[str, str, t.Callable[[], bool]]:
-        pieces = split_template_path(template)
+        pieces = split_template_path(os.path.normpath(template))
         for searchpath in self.searchpath:
             filename = os.path.join(searchpath, *pieces)
             f = open_if_exists(filename)
@@ -210,9 +230,69 @@ class FileSystemLoader(BaseLoader):
                 except OSError:
                     return False
 
+            if self.auto_reload:
+                self._environments.add(environment)
+
             return contents, filename, uptodate
         raise TemplateNotFound(template)
 
+    def _get_template_name(self, file_path: str) -> t.Optional[str]:
+        """Convert an absolute file path back into the template name
+        that would be used to look it up through this loader, or
+        ``None`` if the path isn't inside any of the search paths.
+        """
+        file_path = os.path.abspath(file_path)
+
+        for searchpath in self.searchpath:
+            searchpath = os.path.abspath(searchpath)
+
+            try:
+                common = os.path.commonpath([searchpath, file_path])
+            except ValueError:
+                # Raised on Windows when the paths are on different
+                # drives, meaning the file can't be in this searchpath.
+                continue
+
+            if common != searchpath:
+                continue
+
+            name = os.path.relpath(file_path, searchpath)
+            return name.replace(os.path.sep, "/")
+
+        return None
+
+    def _on_template_changed(self, file_paths: t.List[str]) -> None:
+        """Invalidate cached templates for the given absolute file
+        paths across every environment that uses this loader.
+
+        Call this whenever a template file in one of this loader's
+        search paths is created, modified, or deleted, to proactively
+        drop it from every tracked environment's cache instead of
+        waiting for the next lookup's ``uptodate`` check. This is also
+        useful in tests to simulate a file system change without
+        depending on a real file watcher. Paths that don't correspond
+        to a template inside one of this loader's search paths are
+        silently ignored.
+        """
+        for file_path in file_paths:
+            name = self._get_template_name(file_path)
+
+            if name is None:
+                continue
+
+            cache_key = (weakref.ref(self), name)
+
+            for environment in self._environments:
+                cache = environment.cache
+
+                if cache is None:
+                    continue
+
+                try:
+                    del cache[cache_key]
+                except KeyError:
+                    pass
+
     def list_templates(self) -> t.List[str]:
         found = set()
         for searchpath in self.searchpath:
@@ -326,7 +406,8 @@ class PackageLoader(BaseLoader):
     def
